title: Source registry
description: The fetch allowlist for this project's research gnome (EPIC2-04, platform ADR-0025). Nothing off this registry is ever fetched; adding a source is a one-file PR against this registry. Reference implementation of the studio source-registry convention (platform docs/source-registry.md).

id.description: Stable slug; scratch files and seen-state key off it.

type.description: What the source is: a syndication feed, a page whose links/content change, or a machine-readable index of filings/articles.

url.description: The exact URL the fetch layer requests. The registry is the allowlist — the fetcher requests this URL (plus the host's robots.txt) and nothing else.

parser.description: How the fetch layer itemizes the response (rss covers RSS 2.0 and Atom).

publisher.description: Publisher name as it should appear on signals extracted from this source.

scope.description: What this source covers (region, beat) — for humans reviewing the registry.

status.description: active = fetched on schedule; manual = never fetched (robots/ToS or no machine-readable surface — material arrives via research issues); retired = kept for provenance, never fetched.

notes.description: robots/ToS findings and the compatibility judgment; for manual sources, why they must stay manual.

rate_limit_s.description: Per-host politeness delay; honor any published Crawl-delay.
